role: ShapeHow is the visible, in-rail presentation-preference affordance that lets an operator at a locus reshape how the system shows that thing and records the choice via an onShape callback.
capabilities: Render bare one-click preference chips (terser, more detail)
Render arg-taking chips that arm a text input (lead with…, show as…)
Track armed chip and typed arg in local React state
Submit bare preference with a human-readable text phrase
Submit arg-taking preference only when a non-empty arg is provided
Show busy state with a 'shaping…' badge and disable controls
Highlight the currently active kind with a star marker
Show an EmptyState prompt when no preference is set and no chip is armed
Handle Enter to submit and Escape to cancel the arg input
Use shared kit components (Surface, Badge, EmptyState) and CSS var tokens
substance_why: The file implements a complete, wired UI for the presentation-pref learning loop including chip selection, arg input, busy/empty states, active highlighting, and callback dispatch.
interactions_present: Click a bare chip to record terser/more preference
Click an arg-taking chip to show/hide its input
Type an arg and press Enter or click ✓ to submit lead_with/shape
Press Escape to cancel arg input and close it
See busy feedback while a preference is being saved
See active preference highlighted with a star
interactions_missing: No error/failure feedback if onShape or the backend call fails
No way to clear or remove an existing preference
No data-ui-ref or guidable inspector address (intentionally excluded, flagged as gap)
No capability-registry integration; the four kinds are hardcoded from backend vocabulary
No ARIA state attributes (e.g., aria-pressed) on the toggle chips
No explicit keyboard navigation beyond native tab order
No loading skeleton or disabled reason announcement
reuse_verdict_why: The vocabulary and onShape contract are already aligned with the backend learning loop, so the component should be preserved in behavior and rebuilt with the new design-system form.
decomposition_hints: Extract BareChip and ArgChip as reusable subcomponents
Extract the arg-input form into its own controlled component
Move the PRESENTATION_PREFS vocabulary into a capabilities/registry-driven module instead of hardcoding
Separate presentation state (active, busy, empty) from the preference-recording logic
Encapsulate local arming/submit state in a small useShapeHow hook
